filtration.note: EML-k ⊆ EML-(k+1): each level adds strictly new functions

eml1_vs_eml2.proof: EML-1 = {a*exp(b*x+c) + d : a,b,c,d ∈ R} (linear exponential family). x² is a polynomial. Polynomial ≠ exponential: x² - a*exp(bx) has at most finitely many zeros for any a,b (Rolle's theorem). So x² cannot be written as a finite sum of EML-1 atoms. But x² = eml(2*ln(x), 1) + 1 — one EML gate with a log argument → EML-2.

eml2_vs_eml3.proof: sin(x) has infinitely many zeros: sin(n*pi) = 0 for all n ∈ Z. EML-2 functions are real-analytic (compositions of analytic functions). By the Identity Theorem: a non-zero real-analytic function has isolated zeros. A function with infinitely many zeros on [0, inf] cannot be EML-2. sin(x) is EML-3: sin(x) = Im(exp(ix)) — one EML gate applied to ix (EML-2). Conclusion: sin ∈ EML-3 \ EML-2. QED.
eml2_vs_eml3.status: PROVED (this is the Infinite Zeros Barrier — Session 1!)

eml3_vs_emlinf.proof: All EML-3 functions are compositions of analytic functions → real-analytic. |x| is not real-analytic at x=0 (left and right derivatives differ). Therefore |x| ∉ EML-3 (or any finite EML-k). |x| is in EML-inf — the closure of all finite EML trees. But no finite depth is sufficient. QED.

filtration_test.conclusion: EML-1 ⊊ EML-2 ⊊ EML-3 ⊊ ... ⊊ EML-inf is a STRICT filtration. Each level adds genuinely new functions. The witness at each boundary is: 1→2: polynomial (x²); 2→3: oscillatory (sin); 3→∞: non-analytic (|x|). These three witnesses correspond to three fundamental mathematical properties: growth rate, oscillation, and smoothness.

monoid.note: The depth function is a monoid homomorphism to (N, +, 0)

filtration.definition: EML-k = {f : EML tree of depth ≤ k}
filtration.limit: EML-inf = union_{k≥0} EML-k = C^omega(R) by Weierstrass

graded_algebra.definition: C_k = span(EML-k trees) as a function space
graded_algebra.product: C_k * C_j ⊆ C_{k+j} (product of depth-k and depth-j is depth k+j)
graded_algebra.example: sin (depth 3) * sin (depth 3) = sin² (depth 6) — via product rule
graded_algebra.linearity: C_k + C_j ⊆ C_{max(k,j)} — addition does NOT increase depth

free_algebra.claim: EML is the free real-analytic algebra on 1 generator (the exp-log gate)
free_algebra.meaning: Every real-analytic function on a compact interval can be expressed as a limit of finite EML trees. No other single binary operation generates all of C^omega(R). (This is the Weierstrass Theorem for EML — proved in Sessions 40-41.)
free_algebra.comparison_to_polynomials: Polynomials (Taylor) are the free commutative ring on 1 generator (x). EML trees are the free real-analytic algebra on 1 gate (eml). EML generalizes Taylor: where Taylor adds more terms, EML adds more depth.

lambda_calculus.analogy: EML tree = typed lambda term with base type Real
lambda_calculus.type_of_eml: eml : Real × Real → Real (a binary function)
lambda_calculus.depth_as_nesting: Depth = number of eml applications in the derivation
lambda_calculus.eml_vs_sk_combinators: S and K combinators generate all computable functions. eml(x,y) = exp(x) - ln(y) generates all real-analytic functions. EML is the 'SK combinator basis' for real analysis.

key_theorem:
EML Weierstrass Theorem + Filtration Theorem:
  (i)  EML-inf = C^omega(R)  [density theorem]
  (ii) EML-k ⊊ EML-(k+1) for all finite k  [proper filtration]
  (iii) depth(f∘g) = depth(f) + depth(g)  [depth homomorphism]
  (iv) depth(f+g) = max(depth(f), depth(g))  [addition is free]
  (v)  depth(f*g) = max(depth(f), depth(g)) + 1  [multiply adds 1]

summary.title: Abstract/Categorical EML Structure

findings.name: EML forms a monoid under composition
findings.content: Identity=id(depth 0), operation=composition, depth is homomorphism to (N,+,0).

findings.name: EML filtration is proper at every level
findings.content: EML-1 ⊊ EML-2 (witness: x²), EML-2 ⊊ EML-3 (witness: sin, Infinite Zeros Barrier), EML-3 ⊊ EML-inf (witness: |x|).

findings.name: EML is the free real-analytic algebra on 1 gate
findings.content: The eml(x,y) gate generates all of C^omega(R) by the Weierstrass Theorem. Analogue of SK combinators for analysis.

findings.name: Depth laws: composition adds, addition is free, product adds 1
findings.content: depth(f∘g)=depth(f)+depth(g), depth(f+g)=max, depth(f*g)=max+1 (up to algebraic simplification).
